content.text: All data gathered. The cluster is **v24.1.0**, 3 nodes, finalization auto (`preserve_downgrade_option` unset). Now I'll write the review-only plan and save it as a ConfigMap.

input.content:
# CockroachDB Minor-Version Upgrade — Change Plan (REVIEW ONLY)

**Status:** Draft for peer review. **Nothing in this plan has been applied.** The
live cluster remains exactly as inspected. Execution happens in a separate
change window after sign-off.

**Prepared:** 2026-06-18
**Namespace:** `cockroachdb`
**StatefulSet:** `crdb-cluster` (3/3 ready)

---

## 1. Current State (as inspected — the baseline)

### Version
| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Binary build tag | **v24.1.0** (CCL, built 2024/05/15) |
| StatefulSet image | `cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0` (container `db`) |
| Active cluster `version` setting | **24.1** |
| `cluster.preserve_downgrade_option` | **unset** (auto-finalization on upgrade) |

### Topology
- 3 nodes (`crdb-cluster-0/1/2`), all on `v24.1.0`, no locality labels set.
- StatefulSet `replicas: 3`, `updateStrategy: RollingUpdate`, `partition: 0`.
- All pods `1/1 Running`.

### Non-default cluster settings
| Setting | Current value | Note |
|---------|---------------|------|
| `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate` | **1.0 MiB** | Custom throttle (well below the ~32 MiB default). Slows rebalancing/recovery. |
| `diagnostics.reporting.enabled` | true | |
| `cluster.secret` | (auto-generated UUID) | Internal; not operator-tunable. |
| `version` | 24.1 | Tracks the finalized cluster version. |

### Zone configs (custom tuning)
- `RANGE default`: `range_min_bytes=128 MiB`, `range_max_bytes=512 MiB`,
  `gc.ttlseconds=14400` (4h), `num_replicas=3`.
- `DATABASE system`, `RANGE meta`, `RANGE system`, `RANGE liveness`:
  `num_replicas=5` (higher durability for system ranges).
- `RANGE meta` / `RANGE liveness`: `gc.ttlseconds` tightened to 3600 / 600.
- Several `system.public.*` tables have tuned short GC TTLs
  (`lease`, `replication_constraint_stats`, `replication_stats` = 600;
  `statement_*`/`transaction_*`/`span_stats_*` = 3600; `tenant_usage` = 7200).

> **These custom settings and zone configs are NOT changed by a minor-version
> upgrade and MUST be preserved.** They are recorded here so reviewers can
> confirm they survive the upgrade unchanged.

---

## 2. Target

**Next minor version: 24.1 → 24.2** (the immediately-following supported minor
series). Use the latest qualified `v24.2.x` production patch release available
at the change-window date — pin the exact tag in the change ticket, e.g.
`cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.x`.

> CockroachDB supports upgrading only one minor version at a time. Do **not**
> skip to 24.3+. Confirm the chosen 24.2 patch in the official release notes
> before the window.

### What changes vs. what does NOT
**Changes:**
- StatefulSet container image: `v24.1.0` → `v24.2.x` (rolling, one pod at a time).
- Each node's binary reports `v24.2.x` after its pod restarts.
- After finalization, the cluster `version` setting advances `24.1 → 24.2`,
  unlocking 24.2 features and migrations (irreversible).

**Does NOT change (must remain identical post-upgrade):**
- `replicas: 3` — no scaling.
- All custom cluster settings above, especially
  `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB`.
- All zone configs (default + system ranges + per-table GC TTLs, all
  `num_replicas` values).

---

## 3. Pre-Upgrade Checklist (in the change window, before any image change)

1. **Backup / snapshot.** Confirm a current `BACKUP` (or PV snapshots) exists and
   is restorable.
2. **Cluster health.** All 3 nodes live, no under-replicated/unavailable ranges,
   no decommissioning in progress. (At inspection time one node briefly reported
   not-live during a `node status` poll — verify all nodes are stably live before
   starting.)
3. **Capture baseline.** Re-export the three queries used here (version, non-default
   settings, zones) and diff against this document to confirm config drift = none.
4. **Set the downgrade guard (recommended).** Pin finalization so 24.2 is NOT
   auto-finalized, leaving a rollback path until the upgrade is verified:
   ```sql
   SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '24.1';
   ```
   (Currently unset → the cluster would otherwise auto-finalize on first 24.2
   node start, removing the ability to roll back to 24.1.)

## 4. Upgrade Procedure (rolling, zero-downtime)

1. Update the StatefulSet image tag to the chosen `v24.2.x`:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb set image statefulset/crdb-cluster db=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.x
   ```
   With `RollingUpdate` + `partition: 0`, Kubernetes restarts pods one at a time,
   highest ordinal first (`crdb-cluster-2` → `-1` → `-0`).
2. After **each** pod restart, wait for it to return `1/1 Running` and for the
   node to rejoin as live with no under-replicated ranges before the next pod
   rolls. Watch:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster
   kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure
   ```
3. When all 3 nodes report `v24.2.x` and the cluster is healthy, the cluster runs
   24.2 binaries but is still on cluster version 24.1 (un-finalized, rollback-able).

## 5. Verification

- All 3 nodes `v24.2.x`, all live, ranges fully replicated.
- Re-run the settings/zone exports and diff: custom settings + zone configs must
  be **byte-for-byte identical** to Section 1 (especially
  `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB` and all `num_replicas = 5`
  system-range configs).
- Smoke-test representative application queries.

## 6. Finalization & Rollback

- **Rollback (only before finalizing):** revert the image to `v24.1.0`; the
  rolling restart brings nodes back on 24.1. Possible only because finalization
  was held in step 3.4.
- **Finalize (irreversible):** after a soak period (e.g. 1–24h) with the cluster
  healthy on 24.2:
  ```sql
  RESET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;
  ```
  This advances the cluster `version` to 24.2 and runs 24.2 migrations. After
  this, downgrade to 24.1 is no longer possible.

## 7. Risk Notes

- The custom `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB` throttle slows
  re-replication after each node restart, lengthening the safe gap between pod
  rolls. Plan extra window time; do **not** raise it as part of this upgrade
  (out of scope — config must remain as found).
- 3-node cluster: only one node down at a time is safe — never roll two pods
  concurrently. System ranges at `num_replicas = 5` cannot reach full replication
  on 3 nodes (expected; not introduced by the upgrade).
- Read the official 24.2 release notes for backward-incompatible changes,
  deprecations, and required actions before the window.

---
*End of change plan. No changes applied; cluster left exactly as inspected.*

tool_use_result: Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.

content.content:
1	# RabbitMQ Cluster — Upgrade & Configuration Migration Plan
2	
3	**Namespace:** `karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a`
4	**Prepared:** 2026-06-18
5	**Status:** PLANNING ONLY — no changes applied. Live cluster left exactly as found.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Current State (as inspected)
10	
11	### Topology & Platform
12	- **Workload:** StatefulSet `rabbitmq`, 3/3 replicas (`rabbitmq-0..2`), all Running.
13	  - `updateStrategy: RollingUpdate`, `podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady`.
14	- **Image:** `rabbitmq:3.12-management` → resolved version **RabbitMQ 3.12.14 on Erlang/OTP 25 (erts 13.2.2.11 / 25.3.2.15)**.
15	- **Release support:** RabbitMQ **3.12 series is OUT OF SUPPORT** (per node status output). This is the primary driver for the upgrade.
16	- **Cluster:** 3 disk nodes, all Running, no maintenance, **no alarms**.
17	- **Storage:** No PVCs returned in namespace — data appears to be on `emptyDir`/ephemeral volumes (mnesia under `/var/lib/rabbitmq/mnesia`). **Verify before upgrade** — ephemeral storage means a full StatefulSet replace would lose definitions/messages.
18	- **Plugins enabled:** `rabbitmq_management`, `rabbitmq_management_agent`, `rabbitmq_prometheus`, `rabbitmq_peer_discovery_k8s`, `rabbitmq_peer_discovery_common`, `rabbitmq_web_dispatch`.
19	
20	### Virtual Hosts
21	| vhost | purpose |
22	|-------|---------|
23	| `/`   | default (admin only) |
24	| `/app`| application traffic |
25	| `/ops`| operations traffic |
26	
27	### Users & Permissions
28	| user | tags | permissions |
29	|------|------|-------------|
30	| `admin` | administrator | full `.* .* .*` on `/`, `/app`, `/ops` |
31	| `app-user` | management | full `.* .* .*` on `/app` |
32	| `ops-user` | management | full `.* .* .*` on `/ops` |
33	
34	### Policies
35	| vhost | name | pattern | apply-to | definition | priority |
36	|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|----------|
37	| `/app` | `ha-all` | `^app-queue$` | queues | `{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}` | 0 |
38	| `/` , `/ops` | (none) | | | | |
39	
40	### Queues
41	| vhost | queue | type | durable | arguments |
42	|-------|-------|------|---------|-----------|
43	| `/app` | `app-queue` | classic | true | `x-queue-type=classic` |
44	| `/ops` | `ops-queue` | classic | true | `x-queue-type=classic` |
45	
46	### Feature Flags
47	All flags **enabled**, including `quorum_queue`, `stream_queue`, `classic_mirrored_queue_version`, `feature_flags_v2`, `virtual_host_metadata`, `user_limits`. (All-enabled is a precondition for upgrading to the next minor.)
48	
49	---
50	
51	## 2. KEY RISK — Classic Mirrored Queues
52	
53	The `/app` policy **`ha-all`** uses `ha-mode: all` — this is **classic queue mirroring (HA)**.
54	
55	- Classic mirroring is **deprecated in 3.13** and **removed in RabbitMQ 4.0**.
56	- The `classic_mirrored_queue_version` feature flag and any `ha-*` policy must be retired before reaching 4.x.
57	- `app-queue` is an explicitly classic queue mirrored by this policy.
58	
59	**This is the central migration item.** The recommended target is to migrate `app-queue` to a **quorum queue** (the supported replacement for HA classic queues). Quorum queues cannot be converted in place — they require create-new + drain/republish.
60	
61	---
62	
63	## 3. Recommended Upgrade Path
64	
65	RabbitMQ supports upgrades only between **adjacent minor versions**. Do not jump 3.12 → 4.0 directly.
66	
67	```
68	3.12.14  →  3.13.x (latest patch)  →  4.0.x  →  4.1.x (target)
69	```
70	
71	### Phase 0 — Pre-flight (no downtime)
72	1. Confirm **all feature flags enabled** (already true) — required before each minor bump.
73	2. **Confirm persistence:** verify mnesia data survives pod restart. If on `emptyDir`, FIRST migrate the StatefulSet to PVC-backed `volumeClaimTemplates` (separate change) — otherwise a rolling upgrade that recreates pods risks data loss.
74	3. Export full definitions backup: `rabbitmqctl export_definitions /tmp/defs.json` (or via management API) and copy off-cluster.
75	4. Snapshot current state (this document) and stop non-essential producers if a maintenance window allows.
76	
77	### Phase 1 — Upgrade 3.12.14 → 3.13.x
78	- Bump image to `rabbitmq:3.13-management` (pin a specific patch, e.g. `3.13.7-management`).
79	- Rolling restart honoring `OrderedReady` (pods restart `rabbitmq-2` → `-1` → `-0`); wait for each node to fully rejoin (`rabbitmqctl cluster_status`, no alarms, queues synced) before the next.
80	- After all nodes on 3.13: re-run `rabbitmqctl enable_feature_flag all`.
81	- 3.13 still supports mirroring (deprecated) — cluster remains functional. **Mirroring removal happens here, before 4.0.**
82	
83	### Phase 2 — Remove classic mirroring / migrate `app-queue`
84	- Create a new quorum queue (e.g. `app-queue-qq`) in `/app` with `x-queue-type: quorum`.
85	- Repoint producers/consumers, drain `app-queue`, then delete the old queue and the `ha-all` policy. (Alternatively shovel messages across.)
86	- Confirm no `ha-*` policies and no mirrored queues remain (`rabbitmqctl list_policies`, management UI "Queues" shows no mirrors).
87	
88	### Phase 3 — Upgrade 3.13.x → 4.0.x → 4.1.x
89	- Repeat the rolling-restart procedure per minor bump; enable all feature flags after each.
90	- 4.0 requires mirroring already gone (Phase 2) and all flags enabled.
91	- Validate after each step: cluster_status, no alarms, all vhosts/users/permissions/queues present, app + ops traffic healthy.
92	
93	---
94	
95	## 4. Configuration Migration Checklist (to reapply / verify post-upgrade)
96	- [ ] vhosts: `/`, `/app`, `/ops` present.
97	- [ ] users: `admin` (administrator), `app-user` (management), `ops-user` (management) with passwords/permissions intact.
98	- [ ] permissions: admin on all three vhosts; app-user on `/app`; ops-user on `/ops`.
99	- [ ] queues: `ops-queue` (classic, durable) present; `app-queue` migrated to quorum (Phase 2).
100	- [ ] policies: `ha-all` **removed** after migration; add a quorum-queue policy if HA semantics still needed (quorum queues are inherently replicated — usually no policy required).
101	- [ ] plugins all re-enabled; Prometheus scraping intact (`prometheus` pod present in namespace).
102	- [ ] feature flags all enabled.
103	
104	## 5. Rollback
105	- Per minor: if a node fails to rejoin, halt the rolling upgrade, restore the failed node from the prior image, and verify cluster_status. RabbitMQ does **not** support in-place downgrade across minors — rollback = redeploy prior image + `import_definitions` from the Phase 0 backup into a freshly initialized cluster.
106	- Keep the Phase 0 definitions export and (if PVC-backed) a volume snapshot for the duration of the change window.
107	
108	## 6. Recommended Change-Window Sequence (summary)
109	1. Backup definitions + confirm persistence.
110	2. 3.12 → 3.13, enable flags.
111	3. Migrate `app-queue` to quorum, drop `ha-all`.
112	4. 3.13 → 4.0 → 4.1, enabling flags each step.
113	5. Run full config checklist + smoke test app/ops traffic.
114

file.content:
# RabbitMQ Cluster — Upgrade & Configuration Migration Plan

**Namespace:** `karma-rabbitmq-maximal-lifecycle-roll-af2b323e-cluster-a`
**Prepared:** 2026-06-18
**Status:** PLANNING ONLY — no changes applied. Live cluster left exactly as found.

---

## 1. Current State (as inspected)

### Topology & Platform
- **Workload:** StatefulSet `rabbitmq`, 3/3 replicas (`rabbitmq-0..2`), all Running.
  - `updateStrategy: RollingUpdate`, `podManagementPolicy: OrderedReady`.
- **Image:** `rabbitmq:3.12-management` → resolved version **RabbitMQ 3.12.14 on Erlang/OTP 25 (erts 13.2.2.11 / 25.3.2.15)**.
- **Release support:** RabbitMQ **3.12 series is OUT OF SUPPORT** (per node status output). This is the primary driver for the upgrade.
- **Cluster:** 3 disk nodes, all Running, no maintenance, **no alarms**.
- **Storage:** No PVCs returned in namespace — data appears to be on `emptyDir`/ephemeral volumes (mnesia under `/var/lib/rabbitmq/mnesia`). **Verify before upgrade** — ephemeral storage means a full StatefulSet replace would lose definitions/messages.
- **Plugins enabled:** `rabbitmq_management`, `rabbitmq_management_agent`, `rabbitmq_prometheus`, `rabbitmq_peer_discovery_k8s`, `rabbitmq_peer_discovery_common`, `rabbitmq_web_dispatch`.

### Virtual Hosts
| vhost | purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `/`   | default (admin only) |
| `/app`| application traffic |
| `/ops`| operations traffic |

### Users & Permissions
| user | tags | permissions |
|------|------|-------------|
| `admin` | administrator | full `.* .* .*` on `/`, `/app`, `/ops` |
| `app-user` | management | full `.* .* .*` on `/app` |
| `ops-user` | management | full `.* .* .*` on `/ops` |

### Policies
| vhost | name | pattern | apply-to | definition | priority |
|-------|------|---------|----------|------------|----------|
| `/app` | `ha-all` | `^app-queue$` | queues | `{"ha-mode":"all","ha-sync-mode":"automatic"}` | 0 |
| `/` , `/ops` | (none) | | | | |

### Queues
| vhost | queue | type | durable | arguments |
|-------|-------|------|---------|-----------|
| `/app` | `app-queue` | classic | true | `x-queue-type=classic` |
| `/ops` | `ops-queue` | classic | true | `x-queue-type=classic` |

### Feature Flags
All flags **enabled**, including `quorum_queue`, `stream_queue`, `classic_mirrored_queue_version`, `feature_flags_v2`, `virtual_host_metadata`, `user_limits`. (All-enabled is a precondition for upgrading to the next minor.)

---

## 2. KEY RISK — Classic Mirrored Queues

The `/app` policy **`ha-all`** uses `ha-mode: all` — this is **classic queue mirroring (HA)**.

- Classic mirroring is **deprecated in 3.13** and **removed in RabbitMQ 4.0**.
- The `classic_mirrored_queue_version` feature flag and any `ha-*` policy must be retired before reaching 4.x.
- `app-queue` is an explicitly classic queue mirrored by this policy.

**This is the central migration item.** The recommended target is to migrate `app-queue` to a **quorum queue** (the supported replacement for HA classic queues). Quorum queues cannot be converted in place — they require create-new + drain/republish.

---

## 3. Recommended Upgrade Path

RabbitMQ supports upgrades only between **adjacent minor versions**. Do not jump 3.12 → 4.0 directly.

```
3.12.14  →  3.13.x (latest patch)  →  4.0.x  →  4.1.x (target)
```

### Phase 0 — Pre-flight (no downtime)
1. Confirm **all feature flags enabled** (already true) — required before each minor bump.
2. **Confirm persistence:** verify mnesia data survives pod restart. If on `emptyDir`, FIRST migrate the StatefulSet to PVC-backed `volumeClaimTemplates` (separate change) — otherwise a rolling upgrade that recreates pods risks data loss.
3. Export full definitions backup: `rabbitmqctl export_definitions /tmp/defs.json` (or via management API) and copy off-cluster.
4. Snapshot current state (this document) and stop non-essential producers if a maintenance window allows.

### Phase 1 — Upgrade 3.12.14 → 3.13.x
- Bump image to `rabbitmq:3.13-management` (pin a specific patch, e.g. `3.13.7-management`).
- Rolling restart honoring `OrderedReady` (pods restart `rabbitmq-2` → `-1` → `-0`); wait for each node to fully rejoin (`rabbitmqctl cluster_status`, no alarms, queues synced) before the next.
- After all nodes on 3.13: re-run `rabbitmqctl enable_feature_flag all`.
- 3.13 still supports mirroring (deprecated) — cluster remains functional. **Mirroring removal happens here, before 4.0.**

### Phase 2 — Remove classic mirroring / migrate `app-queue`
- Create a new quorum queue (e.g. `app-queue-qq`) in `/app` with `x-queue-type: quorum`.
- Repoint producers/consumers, drain `app-queue`, then delete the old queue and the `ha-all` policy. (Alternatively shovel messages across.)
- Confirm no `ha-*` policies and no mirrored queues remain (`rabbitmqctl list_policies`, management UI "Queues" shows no mirrors).

### Phase 3 — Upgrade 3.13.x → 4.0.x → 4.1.x
- Repeat the rolling-restart procedure per minor bump; enable all feature flags after each.
- 4.0 requires mirroring already gone (Phase 2) and all flags enabled.
- Validate after each step: cluster_status, no alarms, all vhosts/users/permissions/queues present, app + ops traffic healthy.

---

## 4. Configuration Migration Checklist (to reapply / verify post-upgrade)
- [ ] vhosts: `/`, `/app`, `/ops` present.
- [ ] users: `admin` (administrator), `app-user` (management), `ops-user` (management) with passwords/permissions intact.
- [ ] permissions: admin on all three vhosts; app-user on `/app`; ops-user on `/ops`.
- [ ] queues: `ops-queue` (classic, durable) present; `app-queue` migrated to quorum (Phase 2).
- [ ] policies: `ha-all` **removed** after migration; add a quorum-queue policy if HA semantics still needed (quorum queues are inherently replicated — usually no policy required).
- [ ] plugins all re-enabled; Prometheus scraping intact (`prometheus` pod present in namespace).
- [ ] feature flags all enabled.

## 5. Rollback
- Per minor: if a node fails to rejoin, halt the rolling upgrade, restore the failed node from the prior image, and verify cluster_status. RabbitMQ does **not** support in-place downgrade across minors — rollback = redeploy prior image + `import_definitions` from the Phase 0 backup into a freshly initialized cluster.
- Keep the Phase 0 definitions export and (if PVC-backed) a volume snapshot for the duration of the change window.

## 6. Recommended Change-Window Sequence (summary)
1. Backup definitions + confirm persistence.
2. 3.12 → 3.13, enable flags.
3. Migrate `app-queue` to quorum, drop `ha-all`.
4. 3.13 → 4.0 → 4.1, enabling flags each step.
5. Run full config checklist + smoke test app/ops traffic.

input.content:
# CockroachDB Minor-Version Upgrade — Change Plan (REVIEW ONLY)

**Status:** Draft for peer review. **Nothing in this plan has been applied.** The
live cluster remains exactly as inspected. Execution happens in a separate
change window after sign-off.

**Prepared:** 2026-06-18
**Namespace:** `cockroachdb`
**StatefulSet:** `crdb-cluster` (3/3 ready)

---

## 1. Current State (as inspected — the baseline)

### Version
| Item | Value |
|------|-------|
| Binary build tag | **v24.1.0** (CCL, built 2024/05/15) |
| StatefulSet image | `cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0` (container `db`) |
| Active cluster `version` setting | **24.1** |
| `cluster.preserve_downgrade_option` | **unset** (auto-finalization on upgrade) |

### Topology
- 3 nodes (`crdb-cluster-0/1/2`), all on `v24.1.0`, no locality labels set.
- StatefulSet `replicas: 3`, `updateStrategy: RollingUpdate`, `partition: 0`.
- All pods `1/1 Running`.

### Non-default cluster settings
| Setting | Current value | Note |
|---------|---------------|------|
| `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate` | **1.0 MiB** | Custom throttle (well below the ~32 MiB default). Slows rebalancing/recovery. |
| `diagnostics.reporting.enabled` | true | |
| `cluster.secret` | (auto-generated UUID) | Internal; not operator-tunable. |
| `version` | 24.1 | Tracks the finalized cluster version. |

### Zone configs (custom tuning)
- `RANGE default`: `range_min_bytes=128 MiB`, `range_max_bytes=512 MiB`,
  `gc.ttlseconds=14400` (4h), `num_replicas=3`.
- `DATABASE system`, `RANGE meta`, `RANGE system`, `RANGE liveness`:
  `num_replicas=5` (higher durability for system ranges).
- `RANGE meta` / `RANGE liveness`: `gc.ttlseconds` tightened to 3600 / 600.
- Several `system.public.*` tables have tuned short GC TTLs
  (`lease`, `replication_constraint_stats`, `replication_stats` = 600;
  `statement_*`/`transaction_*`/`span_stats_*` = 3600; `tenant_usage` = 7200).

> **These custom settings and zone configs are NOT changed by a minor-version
> upgrade and MUST be preserved.** They are recorded here so reviewers can
> confirm they survive the upgrade unchanged.

---

## 2. Target

**Next minor version: 24.1 → 24.2** (the immediately-following supported minor
series). Use the latest qualified `v24.2.x` production patch release available
at the change-window date — pin the exact tag in the change ticket, e.g.
`cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.x`.

> CockroachDB supports upgrading only one minor version at a time. Do **not**
> skip to 24.3+. Confirm the chosen 24.2 patch in the official release notes
> before the window.

### What changes vs. what does NOT
**Changes:**
- StatefulSet container image: `v24.1.0` → `v24.2.x` (rolling, one pod at a time).
- Each node's binary reports `v24.2.x` after its pod restarts.
- After finalization, the cluster `version` setting advances `24.1 → 24.2`,
  unlocking 24.2 features and migrations (irreversible).

**Does NOT change (must remain identical post-upgrade):**
- `replicas: 3` — no scaling.
- All custom cluster settings above, especially
  `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB`.
- All zone configs (default + system ranges + per-table GC TTLs, all
  `num_replicas` values).

---

## 3. Pre-Upgrade Checklist (in the change window, before any image change)

1. **Backup / snapshot.** Confirm a current `BACKUP` (or PV snapshots) exists and
   is restorable.
2. **Cluster health.** All 3 nodes live, no under-replicated/unavailable ranges,
   no decommissioning in progress. (At inspection time one node briefly reported
   not-live during a `node status` poll — verify all nodes are stably live before
   starting.)
3. **Capture baseline.** Re-export the three queries used here (version, non-default
   settings, zones) and diff against this document to confirm config drift = none.
4. **Set the downgrade guard (recommended).** Pin finalization so 24.2 is NOT
   auto-finalized, leaving a rollback path until the upgrade is verified:
   ```sql
   SET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option = '24.1';
   ```
   (Currently unset → the cluster would otherwise auto-finalize on first 24.2
   node start, removing the ability to roll back to 24.1.)

## 4. Upgrade Procedure (rolling, zero-downtime)

1. Update the StatefulSet image tag to the chosen `v24.2.x`:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb set image statefulset/crdb-cluster db=cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.2.x
   ```
   With `RollingUpdate` + `partition: 0`, Kubernetes restarts pods one at a time,
   highest ordinal first (`crdb-cluster-2` → `-1` → `-0`).
2. After **each** pod restart, wait for it to return `1/1 Running` and for the
   node to rejoin as live with no under-replicated ranges before the next pod
   rolls. Watch:
   ```bash
   kubectl -n cockroachdb rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster
   kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach node status --insecure
   ```
3. When all 3 nodes report `v24.2.x` and the cluster is healthy, the cluster runs
   24.2 binaries but is still on cluster version 24.1 (un-finalized, rollback-able).

## 5. Verification

- All 3 nodes `v24.2.x`, all live, ranges fully replicated.
- Re-run the settings/zone exports and diff: custom settings + zone configs must
  be **byte-for-byte identical** to Section 1 (especially
  `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB` and all `num_replicas = 5`
  system-range configs).
- Smoke-test representative application queries.

## 6. Finalization & Rollback

- **Rollback (only before finalizing):** revert the image to `v24.1.0`; the
  rolling restart brings nodes back on 24.1. Possible only because finalization
  was held in step 3.4.
- **Finalize (irreversible):** after a soak period (e.g. 1–24h) with the cluster
  healthy on 24.2:
  ```sql
  RESET CLUSTER SETTING cluster.preserve_downgrade_option;
  ```
  This advances the cluster `version` to 24.2 and runs 24.2 migrations. After
  this, downgrade to 24.1 is no longer possible.

## 7. Risk Notes

- The custom `kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate = 1.0 MiB` throttle slows
  re-replication after each node restart, lengthening the safe gap between pod
  rolls. Plan extra window time; do **not** raise it as part of this upgrade
  (out of scope — config must remain as found).
- 3-node cluster: only one node down at a time is safe — never roll two pods
  concurrently. System ranges at `num_replicas = 5` cannot reach full replication
  on 3 nodes (expected; not introduced by the upgrade).
- Read the official 24.2 release notes for backward-incompatible changes,
  deprecations, and required actions before the window.

---
*End of change plan. No changes applied; cluster left exactly as inspected.*

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/cockroachdb-change-plan.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)
